==================================================================== DAY 9 EXECUTIVE NARRATIVE — INVENTORY POLICY RESULTS Regional Demand Forecasting + Inventory Optimization Author  : arvindswami014uk Date    : 2026-04-09 ====================================================================  EXECUTIVE SUMMARY ────────────────────────────────────────────────── Day 9 translated XGBoost ML forecasts (WMAPE = 11.06%) into actionable inventory policy for all 125 SKU-Region combinations across 5 regions and 6 product categories. Safety stock was calculated using the formula:   SS = Z x sigma_error x sqrt(lead_time) Reorder points were set to protect against stockouts during replenishment lead time

Sustainability Narrative — Carbon-Aware Allocation and Green-Fast Fulfillment  This project uses a synthetic carbon-emissions proxy for comparative planning analysis. Because confirmed shipment distance and measured emissions data were not available in the working dataset, transparent lane-based factors were used instead of claiming precise carbon accounting.  The sustainability logic is operational. Same-region fulfillment is treated as lower-carbon and typically faster than unnecessary cross-region movement. Cross-region lanes are assigned progressively higher carbon factors as lead time increases.  This enables a direct comparison between cost-to-serve and carbon-to-serve. It also makes s

Approach This capstone combined three decision layers: demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and strategic fulfillment scenario analysis. - Built an end-to-end regional demand forecasting and inventory optimization capstone in Python, connecting ML forecasting, inventory policy, and fulfillment strategy decisions. - Improved forecast accuracy from 19.25% WMAPE (best baseline MA7) to 11.06% WMAPE using XGBoost, delivering a 42.5% improvement versus baseline. - Engineered forecasting outputs into safety stock and reorder point policies across 125 SKU-region combinations, quantifying \$157.3K safety stock and \$780.9K replenishment need. - Designed a scenario-based fulfillment strategy framework comparing service, cost, same-region routing, and synthetic carbon trade-offs for executive decision-making. - Produced portfolio-grade

Regional Demand Forecasting + Inventory Optimization Developed an end-to-end supply chain analytics capstone in Python and Google Colab that improved regional demand forecast accuracy, translated forecasts into inventory policies, and evaluated fulfillment strategy trade-offs across service, cost, and sustainability proxy metrics. XGBoost reduced WMAPE from 19.25% to 11.06%, while downstream inventory logic quantified safety stock, storage cost, and replenishment requirements. Final deliverables included executive-ready recommendations, governance documentation, and presentation materials.

Day 13 Interview Q&A  Q: Why did you use WMAPE? A: WMAPE is business-friendly for demand forecasting because it weights error by volume and avoids overemphasizing low-volume series.  Q: Why was XGBoost chosen over Prophet? A: XGBoost achieved the best WMAPE at 11.06% and captured richer feature interactions from engineered demand signals.  Q: How did you connect forecasting to business action? A: I converted forecasts into safety stock and reorder point policies, then used those outputs to inform broader fulfillment strategy decisions.  Q: What is the biggest limitation? A: The carbon metric is a synthetic proxy for comparative strategy evaluation and should not be treated as an audited ESG
